mode_rationale: Pre-flight: Operator-Directive Discovery picked up FUSE-stale DIRECTIVE-20260531-004 (PROP-066 implement) as pending, but cross-check against the clone (source of truth) revealed it was already completed at 08:42Z by commits 29df258c + 5acd56fe + fb7dbcb2 from earlier in the morning. Re-scan in the clone returned two genuinely-pending medium-priority directives: DIRECTIVE-005 (PROP-068 author, filed 07:15Z amended 07:50Z) and DIRECTIVE-006 (PROP-069 author, filed 08:30Z). Selected -005 — filed first, shares root cause with just-deployed PROP-066, operator already did most of the diagnostic legwork. -006 (PROP-069 narrative-cite recalibration) deferred to next tinker run.

mode_2_infrastructure_fuse.evidence: 6 workspace files stale vs origin/main (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, build-scripts/digest-reviews.js, build-scripts/generate-html.js, docs/index.html, monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js). This is precisely the bug class PROP-066 (deployed 08:42Z this morning) is designed to fix: workspace-sync's last cycle was 08:21Z, before PROP-066 landed, so the helper script never invoked. Next workspace-sync cycle (~09:03Z) will execute Step 4c via the new helper and detect upstream-newer-than-last-sentinel-ts, triggering 'node build.js sync-workspace' and resolving all 6 stale files. NOT a manual-action item — self-resolves within ~1h. Disk: project-induced footprint 287MB (<500MB warn). Root filesystem 95% used (488M free) — same as recent cycles; not a tinker-actionable disk-pressure event.

findings.title: 3 agents share workspace-sync's separate-trailing-cleanup-block pattern
findings.description: Per the PROP-068 audit (13 enabled scheduled agents): workspace-sync (CONFIRMED LEAKING ~43%), sloppytoppy-score (RISK if re-enabled, currently disabled), and sloppytoppy-rewrite (RISK if re-enabled, currently disabled) all have a Cleanup bash block separated from their last-push bash block by 32-71 lines of prose. Three more agents (decider line 1029, tinker line 385, structure-integrity line 620) have cleanup blocks far from their main work but no in-prompt push to reorder against; risk-class is the same but the fix shape differs. Curmudgeon and curmudgeon-verify are INVESTIGATE-first pending operator orphan-grep.
findings.operator_action_required: PROP-068 Q-OP-3 (simultaneous fix for sloppytoppy pair) and Q-OP-4 (operator-side grep for curmudgeon/decider/tinker orphans before applying preventive fix).

findings.title: 6 workspace files stale vs origin/main; awaiting first post-PROP-066 workspace-sync cycle
findings.description: Mode 2 quick-check detected 6 stale FUSE files. Cause: workspace-sync last cycle (08:21Z) preceded PROP-066's deployment (08:42Z), so the Step 4c helper-script extraction never invoked. Next cycle (~09:03Z) will be the first to run with PROP-066's helper. If the helper executes as designed, it should detect upstream-newer-than-last-sentinel-ts (last sentinel was 2026-05-30T01:16:37Z from PROP-064 bootstrap; origin/main has dozens of commits since), classify as 'local-ancestor-of-remote', trigger 'node build.js sync-workspace', and write the first post-PROP-066 sync-workspace-runs sentinel.
findings.verification_planned: Next tinker run should check: (a) ls monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T09*.json — expect ≥1; (b) re-run the md5 spot-check on the 6 stale files — expect all match. If sentinel exists but staleness persists, that's a regression and indicates the PROP-066 helper has a path-resolution bug — file PROP-067 (slot reuse since current PROP-067 is superseded).
findings.operator_action_required: None for now — let the canary play out on the natural cadence.
